question_text: Name one way you could make an electromagnet stronger.
correct_answer: Increase the number of coils in the wire, increase the current flowing through it, or use a stronger iron core.
explanation: An electromagnet's strength depends on three main factors: the number of coils (more coils = stronger field), the current flowing through the wire (higher current = stronger field), and the core material (iron is a better core than air). Changing any of these will make the electromagnet stronger.
